body: Ref aside, the Egyptians are a class above NZ and the result is what you would expect. Kiwis showed a lot of fight in the first half, but lost concentration in the second. Egypt just too good. Entertaining game, Ty both teams. Grats Egypt!
label: hot_take

body:
Really impressed by Egypt's passing in this game. Lots of nifty little combinations in small spaces. Very quick and precise. That second goal especially had a beautiful passing sequence. Even the corner for the third goal happened after such a sequence.


Not sure what happened with New Zealand. First half was quite good and showed they could hold their own. But second half they completely switched off.  Egypt's third goal was one of the worst defended corners I've ever seen. Trezegeut didn't even need to jump, let alone move. He was completely open and unmarked for like 5 seconds before the corner was taken. NZ stopped defending for some reason, even though the scoreline was just at 1-2. Very strange. Killed the game right then and there.
label: analysis

body:
Congrats to Egypt. Very good second half from them

Slightly concerned with how tired we looked in that second half. Fitness levels should have been slightly better I think. But hopefully we can regroup for a good performance against Belgium.
label: hot_take

body:
Welp. I can be hopeful for New Zealand but realistically Belgium has to wake up at some point. 

But Uruguay are the really screwed ones. I don't see them getting a win over Spain, and I don't think three draws is going to cut it.
label: hot_take

body:
Disappointing 2nd half from NZ. Game  
Looked fairly even at half time but Egypt were head and shoulders above us in the 2nd. Congrats on your first WC win Egypt 👏 We’ll have to wait a bit longer for ours.
label: hot_take

body: I have obvious biases but Salah was fantastic in that second half. Refs aside, it felt like every time Egypt was creating chances, Mo was involved
label: hot_take

body: the reffing has been incredible this tournament. im not commenting on this game specifically but letting the teams be physical and play has been awesome
label: hot_take

body: NZ showed no reaction during the 2nd half. No. 19 from NZ came on and his mistakes contributed to two of Egypt's goals, he was awful. The ref was shit, but most calls benefited Egypt because they were at least trying to keep attacking. Still, there was a string of plays where nothing got called for NZ.
label: analysis

body: Yes the ref left a lot of stuff go, but Egypt were the much better team in the second half. New Zealand couldn't live with their intensity. Delighted for Mo, feels insane that this is Egypt's first World Cup win considering some of the teams they've had
label: hot_take

body:
>Really impressed by Egypt's passing in this game. Lots of nifty little combinations in small spaces. Very quick and precise. That second goal especially had a beautiful passing sequence. Even the corner for the third goal happened after such a sequence.

You should check out the Belgium match our passing was even better there and we were just unclinical.

>Egypt's third goal was one of the worst defended corners I've ever seen.

NZ's first goal could provide some competition, Our players were literally marking each other lol
label: analysis

body: I think New Zealand got nervous as shit and thought to “play it safe” to hold on to their goal and it backfired
label: hot_take

body: My GOAT never lost it. Slot is having an awful tournament with pretty much all Liverpool players having a great time
label: hot_take

body: I think NZ looked tired in the 2nd half, which is a kinda shame because their attack has been quite electric going forward
label: hot_take

body: I left after they equalized then didn't see the rest of the game. Not surprised Egypt won. They just looked like a completely different side
label: hot_take

body:
yeah but those have been last defender fouls and pretty clear. it isn't the same and i think you know that.

they very clearly have a directive to call the game differently and have.
label: analysis

body: Thanks and thank you for a great game. I agree your boys looked tired and in the second half I felt like you sat back without a plan, not even a real defensive plan. Coach has to react quicker!
label: analysis

body:
Kudos to New Zealand, I did not expect them to be looking this good and coherent in attack. The ref allowed a lot more fouls that should have been called, but I don't think the Kiwis should be feeling down for this defeat for long. They're looking a lot better than a creaking geriatric Belgium team and they have more than a decent chance at actually causing an upset. 

On the other hand, Egypt were also good to watch unlike in Afcon when I last saw them, they were so dour. Salah's finish aside, he was struggling to influence the game. His pace looked gone and it maybe better for him to move centrally and connect play more like Kane and Messi.
label: analysis

body: Dubious ref calls (or non calls) aside, Kiwis made a nest and went to sleep on the pitch. Meanwhile their goalie gave them no breaks, lobbing the ball straight back into play each time. Egypt played with the possession and energy of Turkiye, except they actually scored.
label: analysis

body: should’ve got an assist literally passed the ball to zizo 1 on 1, keeper got dribbled but he hesitated like crazy and gave all the time in the world for the keeper to follow up with a save
label: analysis

body: Good luck Spain, Uruguay is going to kick the shit out of them all game. They're in full meltdown mode.
label: hot_take

body:
It would be hilarious if Cape Verde finishes top of this group just like how Japan did in 2022.

They're the underdog story of this WC.

Getting 2 draws against former World Champions is no mere coincidence, they have deserved it.
label: hot_take

body: So “dark horse” teams that can make deep runs but still not win the thing is really just a fancy way of saying overated af. Look at Belgium, Türkiye, now Uruguay. lol
label: hot_take

body: That second CV goal was defensive error but the finish still took some doing. Think we’ve got a great chance to not only grab a debut W but a spot in the next round. Think I gotta gamble and schedule the time off!
label: hot_take

body:
Referee should have let that free kick at the last minute happen. 

I don't understand how he ended it there.
label: hot_take

body: Cape Verde (against *Spain* and Uruguay) and Curacao get points and perennial Dark Horse Turkiye get nothing against Australia and Paraguay. Embarrassing.
label: hot_take

body:
Congratulations to Cape Verde on this historic performance.

Being unbeaten against Spain and Uruguay is no mean feat and they have performed exceptionally.

Probably the biggest overachievers of this WC.

I hope they reach the knockouts.
label: hot_take

body: Uruguay screwed up again. Damn how did Bielsa waste the potential of Uruguay is insane. Another 2002 disasterclass.
label: hot_take

body:
if Bielsa is gonna play Valverde at the 10, he's gotta do more than pass the ball around in the back and shoot it into the stands 3 times a game. He's got insane talent and just doesnt feel like showing up for some reason. 

De la Cruz should've been in instead of either Ugarte or Bentacur cuz Valverde is just too conservative in that position.
label: analysis

body: I mean, sure a draw could be enough, but I think they can actually beat Saudia Arabia if they play the way they did today
label: hot_take

body: Turkey is shite, but Uruguay and Belgium do have quality. Uruguay just has a bunch of hot headed dumbasses on their squad too. I think Belgium is going to come to play finally against New Zealand (unfortunately because I'd like to see the Kiwis advance... hopefully they get the win tonight!)
label: hot_take

body: When the United States beat the Uruguay 5-1 a lot of people in the comments were saying oh well it’s just a friendly. That should have been Uruguay wake up call but it wasn’t.

body: Cape verde give me what Brazil used to a long time ago now, they give me that fix im looking for of crazy audacious plays, amazing bits of skill and mavericks.
label: hot_take

body:
If you fell Bielsa is stupid, imagine Parrera who wasted a squad with Prime Ronaldinho, Ronaldo Phenomenon, Roberto Carlos, Cafu, Dida, Adriano 'The Emperor', Kaká, Robinho, Juninho.P and Gilberto Silva on 2006.

Well, at least he did won the damn thing in 94'
label: hot_take

body:

Has it been in the room with us this tournament? Because all I see is that a past-his-best Kevin de Bruyne is still by far their most important outfield player.

> I think Belgium is going to come to play finally against New Zealand

I'm gonna laugh when they get hit by another Chris Wood masterclass
label: hot_take

body: They’re just a mediocre team now. They are probably never winning another World Cup. 2010 was their last chance
label: hot_take

body: the finish required so much composure, and technique too. he hit the ball perfectly while at an angle and not saving much time. i’m so happy for you guys. well done!
label: hot_take

body:
Yeah, but to your point he won in 1994, and the 2006 lost in the quarters against a Zidane led team 

Bielsa had Argentina getting eliminated by Sweden in 2002. And now Uruguay by Cape Verde or Saudi. It’s just not the same
label: hot_take

body: Ecuador makes me so mad. They are tough as fuck in the qualifiers only to implode in group stages every time
label: hot_take

body: I agree, why is Bielsa putting him, Ugarte and Bentacur out there a the same time then? Really idiotic stuff from him
label: hot_take

body: Did you watch the match? Or did you not see Muslera run into free space for no reason on the second goal…
label: analysis

body:
It may be boring romps, but there's a chance at fun.

Spain appears miles better than Uruguay, but Uruguay has to win that game. A draw probably isn't going to be enough. But it's within the realm of possibility that Uruguay can win right?

If they win by 1, and Cabo Verde pulls a 2 goal win, we get Cabo Verde in 1st and Spain in THIRD (almost certainly still qualifying though)
label: hot_take

body: Argentina SUCKED hard on the early 2000's. They lost a Copa America to Brazil with a devastating 3-0 defeat. And Brazil was using their 2nd squad because all the main players refused the NT call-up. They had no Ronaldinho, Kaká, Ronaldo, and still humiliated Argentina.
label: hot_take

body: 17 of Irans 26 players have not played competitive football since early February since the Persian gulf league was canceled bec of the war. Friendlies canceled leading up to the World Cup. Logistical restrictions on days leading up to matches. Oldest squad in the tournament. Kept several top quality players off the score sheet today. Wasn’t the best offensive performance by team Melli, but defensively they were stellar. They deserve so much credit.
label: hot_take

body:
I don't know what everyone's complaining about, I found this match extremely enjoyable.

Iran's defending was insane, and it was curious to see how they dealt with being one man up. Their entire gameplan was to defend and counter, and suddenly they had more spaces?

They clearly didn't trust their luck, and played it (relatively) safe, which I think is totally understandable - even with a man down, Belgium had the players to finish off Iran.

Glad they didn't!

WE NEED IRAN VS USA
label: analysis

body:
their defending was exemplary, especially with such disadvantages   impacting their conditioning. 

BBC radio mentioned they were the last team to travel to North America, and the last to be able to train with the new world cup ball.
label: hot_take

body: I don't think playing a striker that isn't fit and hasn't really played football in a year is good decision...
label: hot_take

body: ITT people who don’t like Belgium and therefore think it’s a shit group. Iran-NZ was a cracker and Egypt-Belgium was a great game and featured a Lukaku aura goal and an absolute banger for Egypt. I expect Egypt’s remaining fixtures will be entertaining as well as the last Belgium game. The draws just show the group is quite evenly matched and the last MD will have plenty of entertainment with Belgium possibly getting grouped.
label: hot_take

body: Their domestic league has been on a slow rise, actually ahead of the Dutch one in UEFA rankings now, and they seem to produce players of decent level quite consistently, so I very much doubt it.
label: hot_take

body:
Lol gtfo Iranian fans boo the anthem because they hate the regime but loudly cheer the players. 

Also, Iran’s disallowed goal was probably one of the smartest set ups of the whole tournament, the scorer should have just waited one second before running.
label: analysis

body: The last solid iran shot was so frustrating to watch as the closest Iranian just stood there butching about not getting the ball when if he had crashed the goal on the shot he would have had an excellent chance at a rebound chance goal, while an Iranian player 10 meters further almost got to the ball. So idiotic that this mindset permeants the game at this level. Dude gets three matches at a world cup in his life, most likely, and he stands around complaining about not getting the ball instead of working harder than the opponent. You see this every match too whether it is people moaning about not getting the ball or rolling around acting like they just got their leg snapped in half(so ridiculous when you compare it to people who actually get hurt, as flailing about when you have a leg broken generally is a bad idea lol) when they could get up and work to make a difference and create space for their team
label: analysis

body: This Belgoul Holden generation really has to be studied lol what a waste. Easiest non host group in the tournament and this is what they do?
label: hot_take

body:
I see so many lesser talented teams play their absolute hearts out and to their credit, they deserve the points.

But it is astounding how shit these world class players can be sometimes and tbf the referees as well, if they don't punish hard fouls, the lesser team will just kill the game and then everyone loses because the better players get fouled constantly.
label: hot_take

body: Belgians are really lucky they got Curtois, otherwise they would of gotten on the first flight back to Brussels
label: hot_take

body: The ref performance was fine. Belgium is just not good. If your team can't win this group or beat an Iranian squad facing so many issues then I don't know what to tell you. You can blame the ref or fouls as your squad keeps under performing.
label: hot_take

body: Like virtually every other improbable draw so far, it came down to players consistently hesitating and failing to capitalize on easy scores.
label: hot_take

body: They played amazing defense.   Had Belgium unable to get anything solid in the box.  Looked like Belgium just isn't good at getting solid shots off, but credit to the defense as well.
label: hot_take

body: Finishing 3rd isn’t an underperformance by any means, especially beating England twice and Brazil in the QFs
label: hot_take

body:
yah its absolute nonsense, we were 6th in market valuation came 3rd but since r/soccer hyped us as "the golden generation" it could never be enough unless we win the WC what no country of our size has done beyond Uruguay 80 years ago.

The narrative can't be turned, it can't be held at a narrative that we are simply good at developing talent for a small country
label: hot_take

body: Thats not underperforming though? Basically each tournament has between 6-4 real contenders. Ending 3d is not underperforming
label: hot_take

body:
I would put it simply: they just don’t have the players they once had. 

They all know, we all know, that their best chance was 2018, and they were quite close to achieving it.
label: hot_take

body:
Obviously, Yamal's starting was a big difference maker, but I think Porro just offers Spain so much dynamism in chance creation down the right side from the fullback position. 

I really like the variation between deep crosses, field switches, underlap runs and overlaps he brings to the side when he plays with Yamal. 

It keeps the defense honest and unable to gamble too much towards stopping any one prong.
label: analysis

body: I feel like that was one of the most boring games I’ve seen and it was 4-0. I get not wanting to over-exert the team, but Spain was legit walking with the ball at times.
label: hot_take

body:
Good game all around. 

I lowkey think Baena-Oyarzabal-Yamal should remain as the starting attack unless Nico can get back to form. Baena was "involved" in every goal, Oyarzabal had a classic "Oyarzabal in a spain kit" performance and Lamine Yamal was cooking on the wing as usual. I also think Lamine Yamal can do even better as he gets more match fit, so thats exciting.

Midfield was great. Controlled the game well. Will be interesting how well they will do against some of the better midfields.

Defense was good. Not much to say since they haven't been really challenged in either of the games. I rate Llorente much higher than Porro but Porro was great today so not gonna complain about starting Porro going forward.

Substitutes were kind of whatever. Since the team had took their foot off the gas, theres not much to say. I hope Llorente gets used as a sub more, he's the most versatile player in the team and brings the most energy off the bench.
label: analysis

body:
A performance like that just reinforces how farcical the whole Saudi Arabian thing has been.

It's clear as day that it's been nothing but lipstick on a pig and that they don't actually care about improving their national sport and just about PR.

Can't wait for them to get 0 points when they host the World Cup
label: hot_take

body:
Beautiful first half, if Lamine and Nico are healthy they can repeat how they played in the Euros

What worries me are De La Fuente experiments and playing players out of positions
label: hot_take

body:
The match basically lasted half an hour, then Spain got off their foot from the gas pedal and cruised to a quite comfortable win without risking injuries.

They're still not the dominant team they were at Euro 2024, but Lamine Yamal and Nico Williams have still to get into full action. Then, we'll see what this team is really capable of.
label: hot_take

body: Spain arrived at the World Cup with many key players injured or out of form due to injuries; the main objective has to be to get everyone back before the knockout stage
label: hot_take

body: De la Fuente look what happens when most of your team is not playing out of position and you actually have some wing threat
label: hot_take

body: If you want to get the best out of Yamal, you need to support him in attack. Porro's style of play works very well with him in that respect, especially since he can invert or overlap depending on situation. Should start against Uruguay.
label: analysis

body: Can we please never play Ferran for the rest of the tournament? Borja would definitely be a better sub than him.
label: hot_take

body: I think even a lot of Saudis are upset that their national league is focusing more on bringing in foreign stars instead of developing local players.
label: hot_take

body:
How much has Saudi Pro League increasing no of foreigners playing actually affected these players performances. They were pretty good last world cup and now they look horrible 

Is lack of play time actually messed up local players. Their keeper seems to be playing in 2nd division

Cape Verde are NOT a good team, Spain should've won even with the line up and formation they put out. Upsets happen all the time, it's not a big deal

label: hot_take

Yes and he's still Spain's best with Lamine. Always scores

label: analysis

body: When it comes to dictating play, it’s not just about controlling the tempo, but about increasing it as well. Rodri attempts plenty of long balls and progressive passes. Frenkie de Jong, on the other hand, mainly controls the tempo without speeding the game up; he prioritizes retaining possession. That shows there are levels to this.
label: analysis

body:
As if watching morota wasn't the proof enough that spain needs to put effort into developing better strikers, feran somehow tops that.

He is a great striker on his day, but he is so inconsistent.
label: hot_take

body: One could argue that opening the league to foreign players make the locals compete for a starting place. Protecting them didn’t work, we were worse pre 2018
label: hot_take

body: i think there's merit in bring foreign stars to Saudi Arabia in order to spread the game/create competition/etc, but the only way to really improve is start sending players to Europe where they will actually develop as players. Japan/Korea have players in mid-tier European leagues toughing it out, not just cruising in the local leagues where they can make lots of money and not improve. USA got better once we started having talent consistently in Europe and not just MLS players
label: analysis

body: Good win, still a lot of practice and getting into form needed. They especially need a threat on the left, something Fermin might have contributed. Starting Olmo was immense, especially against that low block.
label: analysis

body:
Even without key players like Endo, Minamino, Kubo, and Mitoma, we're still competing well. The level of Japanese football keeps improving year by year.

Send as many players as possible to Europe and let them develop there!!!
label: hot_take

body: This Japan team is so likeable! Really good to watch, brilliant attitude, brilliant fans and brilliant results. Of all the teams in the tournament they feel like the one that looks like a club side the most, their patterns of play are so good! Hope they go far!
label: hot_take

body: I don't want to overreact since our knockout path is very difficult...but I think we can beat almost anyone. I have hope we can make a semifinal maybe. The team is playing with amazing fighting spirit.
label: hot_take

body:
Tunisia are nearly always awful at the World Cup yet always qualify with ease. 

They have barely had any good moments, no quality players they just qualify and exist. 

No team has made more World Cup's and done less.

body: Honestly, if you are Tunisia, what do you even do to fix the national team? Feels like they are in an impossible situation. The last generation, who were at least competent, is gone, the new generation is showing nothing and I can't really think of any big prospects. The Federation also looks like a total mess.
label: analysis

body: Nah, their defense is the horrible part and from what I see in two game Japan can dominate the pacing and catch defenders off-guard. I anticipate a goal difference battle between Japan and the Netherlands instead of Japan and Sweden fighting for 3rd.
label: analysis

body:
It is just really funny how the previous coach of Tunesia just decided to play a completely new system at the World Cup. 

Then comes in Renard, who *also* changes up the system. They had three days of practice with Renard! This was a disaster waiting to happen
label: analysis

body: Hopefully I'm not tripping but Japan actually looks stronger than Brazil to me. France and Argentina still stronger but Japan looking mighty strong. Their teamplay is so efficient. No star ego player to look after, nope, just good passing and whoever gets to strike does a good strike. There's no one Ronaldo-like player they feel like they HAVE to pass to, they're just free to pass to the best positioned striker.
label: analysis

body: Regardless of Japan’s results this World Cup. I prefer Hajime Moriyasu to stay as their manager heading to the 2030 World Cup. He has already established the system needed to go head to head with giants.
label: hot_take

body: No. Sweden attack is good, but their defense is really bad. They will get cooked by good teams, just like the game with Netherlands.
label: hot_take

body: They play a style that easily beats other African teams, and gets them into the World Cup. However, their tactics and squad are useless against the rest of the world.
label: hot_take

body: Japan is simply on another level, brilliant to watch, great chemistry ... We may have only seen two matches from them, but i am sure they will advance far

body: Well, we've seen teams who disappointed and couldn't force their luck like Turkey......but Tunisia? That was painful to watch. body: Tunisia better give up their ticket to Nigeria or Cameroon. They are fkn asssss. Worst team out of 48 nations

body: Pretty much this, they were the first african side to ever win a game at the world cup and they have never improved since then. 7 qualifications to the world cup and only 2 wins

body:

I guess getting the bag is gig at the moment.
label: hot_take

body: Correct, we are past the era where we could hold up against a good offence. In order to win against top teams today we will need to score 4 goals minimum.
label: hot_take

body: They have always had the attitude and fans. Now that their hundred years football plan is finally bearing fruit, their technique is catching up as well! So hopefully brilliant result will follow from hereon.
label: hot_take

body:
You have no idea how frustrating it is to be a Tunisian fan.
I fucking hate our national team, we always sucked but never like this, this is the worst showing ever.

Our federation is corrupt, they select players from natinal clubs so clubd get money from Fifa not because they are good. Some players pay to get selected.

I hope we lose by 10-0 against Netherlands so they put those fuckers in prison.
label: hot_take

body:
There's no point in sugarcoating it.  This is a disaster for Ecuador. 

Get the win and pile up a  few extra goals, and you're in decent shape to squeak through as a third place team just so long as Germany doesn't vaporize you too badly.  Instead not only do you not get that precious goal difference, you don't even get the three points. 

  
Better have your bags packed, cause barring a miracle, you're flying home pretty soon.
label: hot_take

body: One crazy stat : Ecuador had more shots on target (15) than Germany (11) against Curaçao. Yet Germany scored 7 goals and Ecuador none...
label: analysis

body:
Everyone complained about the 48 team expanded format and the drop in quality and the fact that nations like Italy and Denmark couldn't make the cut but Curacao and Cabo Verde did. And here we are. We'd never have had these incredible results if Italy and Denmark had been given spots over these little nations. It's definitely not the highest level of football but damn if this isn't what the World Cup is about. These fan and these nations will never forget these results or these performances.

Nor will Ecuador.
label: hot_take

body: People were trashing Ecuador’s finishing, but Curaçaos goalie was just absolute class today. He both was in the right spot at the right time for about half of them and had some incredible saves on a few others.
label: analysis

body:
if curacao can play like this against Ivory Coast
 There could be a chance of making round of 32
That would be insane.
They have their goal and their point. Time to play all or nothing
label: hot_take

body: If you asked a Curacao fan after the draw - Germany, Ivory Coast & an Ecuador side unbeaten since 2024 - would they be happy with a goal, a point and still be in it with a group game to go, they'd snap your hands off.
label: hot_take

body:
They have not made it out of the group stage in 20 years. Also, they seem to consistently have that 1 game in the group stage where they lose in heartbreaking fashion.

2002: Lost 2-1 to Mexico after leading 1-0

2006: N/A as they made it to RO16 without issue

2014: Lost 2-1 to Switzerland at the 90 + 3' via a Seferovic goal 

2022: Losing to Senegal when all they needed was a draw

2026: Losing to Cote D'Ivoire at the death.
label: hot_take

body: Pretty sure Ecuador is cooked. They cant score for shit and now are in a must win situation vs Germany 💀
label: hot_take

body:
Germany might just rest everyone, and still they'll beat ecuador. if Ecuador plays like this, they'll lose and not score a goal. whereas if curacao wants to pull off the improbable, they need to defend and score when given the chances

but, it's looking like it's germany and ivory coast that's going to advance
label: hot_take

body:
Turkey in 2021, Denmark in 2022 and now Turkey & Ecuador in 2026.

I don't want to see the tag of "Dark Horse" ever again.
label: hot_take

body:
Many critics of this format (which I'm critical too) pointed to this group as one where a team could lose 2 games to the "real teams" and still advance in 3rd place just by pouring it on Curacao.

Funny that barring an upset, this going to be one the groups where a 3rd place team doesn't advance
label: hot_take

body: I'm sorry but this was the alleged "future world class team" Ecuador that people were going on about a few years back?
label: hot_take

body:
In the Post Match Thread of Germany v Ivory Coast, I wrote that Curaçao should be a formality for Ivory Coast. But Curaçao's performance makes me rethink the upcoming game. 

Yes Ecuador dominated that game outrageously but Room is by far the MotM. What a game ! Even though some of Ecuador's chances were more towards the GK so it was easier to save some of the shots, still he had some great saves (like the triple one in the 1st half and the corner kick late in the 2nd half).

On the other hand, Enner Valencia's performance was very disappointing. Had he scored in his 1v1 early in the game, that might have been a different story for Ecuador but he didn't. Ecuador keeps being very unlucky with finishing. Against Ivory Coast, they had 2-3 shots which hit the crossbar/post and tonight they had a GK who played out of his mind. 

Curaçao played very well in the few opportunities that he had and could have even snatched a win. What a moment for Curaçao ! Enjoy it !
label: analysis

body: I guess Germany locking up the group tonight is a silver lining for Ecuador since they (Germany) will be more concerned with not collecting injuries or yellow cards than collecting points.
label: hot_take

body:
Ecuador biggest strength was their defense in the qualifiers, they were a rock, allowing only 5 goals in the whole thing. 
The thing is they lack attacking power.
label: analysis

body: A lot of ppl diminished Germanys result against Curacao, but today they executed their match plan perfectly. Their goalie will be a national hero. Even with the loss Equador still has a chance to qualify on last match day, especially since Germany will be rotating.
label: hot_take

body:
Well if you are an Ecuador fan, 4 years ago Brazil, France and Portugal all had nothing to play for on the final matchday and lost to Cameroon, Tunisia, and South Korea respectively. So it's not unthinkable they steal one from a German team that might be playing all their backups and treating it as a friendly.

Nothing I've seen so far makes that seem likely though
label: hot_take

body: Kind of insane how everything is looking up for Curacao, is more likely for them to win or draw than it is for Ecuador to get points against current Germany.
label: hot_take

body:
As long as they dont lose to Curacao they are going through in 2nd.
They are not bad. Struggled in finishing and confidence. They literally had a 2nd goal but the striker decided to have a second touch instead if just shooting the ball
label: analysis

body: It was great, but it lacked excitement because you know that any Ecuadorian becomes a ghost when he enters the penalty area and sends everything straight into the goalkeeper's hands
label: hot_take

body: Admittedly I havent kept up with Ecuador and many other South America teams. But this Ecuador scored 14 goals out of 18 games in their qualifiers (less than a goal per game). Only better than Peru and Chile (tied with Paraguay). How did People think they were a dark horse if they cant score goals?
label: hot_take

body: It's madness for me from a Colombian. I did not even watch the game as I assumed Ecuador would have crushed Curacao with ease. When I turned back it was so shocking. If you couldn't even break down Curacao then how would you even do it versus Germany?
label: hot_take

body: Honestly it was a frustrating game. Amazing work by the Curacao keeper, but the amount of poor crosses by Ecuador pmo
label: hot_take

body: Why Ecuadorians were always shooting straight at the goalkeeper? All 15 shots. That's some low quality striking.
label: hot_take

body: Germany won't drop on this one. Also, their bench is nothing to scoff at. There's hope for Ecuador but I wouldn't be very optimistic.
label: hot_take

body:
Yeah it's a big ask to win that one, this needed to be not just a win but a good one to try and get through with 3 points. 

But you guys are solid at the back and could maybe nick one.
label: hot_take

body: Yes big upset that a tiny Caribbean island is the reason the 2nd place CONMEBOL qualifiers that beat Argentina is likely going out in groups. Does that even have to be spelled out? Come on.
label: hot_take

body: I guess I just think of them a great in conmebol. I guess they were just beating a bad Colombia/brazil/argentina
label: hot_take

body: We have the worst NT of our history, but even at our peak we didn't sell our NT as a potential semifinalist as Ecuadorians wanted to sell it in recent months
label: hot_take

body: Caicedo literally just stands 15 yards from the opponent's penalty box anytime Ecuador has the ball in the box or around the box. He wins the ball when it comes out, yes. But the dude can score goals slightly further forward.
label: analysis

body: Germany wete humiliated in the last two World Cups. I don't see them letting this last group match go even if it's a formality.
label: hot_take

body:
Ivory Coast conceded zero goals in the Afcon qualifiers and they won their last 4 games against France, Ecuador, Scotland and South Korea, conceding only 1 goal.

And yet we still made a comeback beating them. Great team, but we were better. We look good so far, WC is off to a great start but Nagelsmann has got to start Undav eventually.
label: hot_take

body: Germany needs to improve their defence though, it was a mess this game... we were lucky that ivory coast couldnt finish. Felix Nmecha though, what a player
label: hot_take

body: This wasn’t a great performance from Germany. But I do remember not that long ago, a time when we would have lost a game just like this one. So I’ll take it.
label: hot_take

body:
I feel like im going crazy reading the amount of comments acting like Ivory coast controlled this game and got unlucky... 

They scored their one and only chance of the first half and luckily were able to hold onto the lead despite Germany carrying the play the majority of the game... IVC legitimately had two, mayyybe three chances in the whole game yet people are acting like the German defense was scrambling to contain them?

Sometimes I seriously question what match the people here actaully watched...
label: analysis

body:
Great game from both teams. The intensity, the duels, the counters, the nerves... it really felt like a knockout game. 

Congratulations to Germany for their victory. Ivory Coast had countless opportunities to score a 2nd goal but unfortunately for them (fortunately for the Germans) they didn't. Adingra's miss was diabolical. 

When I saw Undav starting to enter the pitch, I knew he would be the X Factor and the turning point of that game. And he delivered ! What a game from him. 

Finally, it would be such a great moment to have Ivory Coast finally go through the GS. Even though their last game on paper against Curaçao should be a formality, we never know what could happen. Also I hope Singo's injury isn't that severe because that might be a huge blow for them. 

All in all, Ivory Coast really showed that they might be a tough team to beat and maybe be a last minute "dark horse" for this WC. They won against us (France) convincingly weeks ago in a friendly. They came back from behind and absolutely walked over us in the 2nd half (although we made several changes in that half). So I knew Ivory Coast would be a team to look for in this WC.
label: analysis

body:
Was at the game, incredibly fun atmosphere 

Thought Germany was way too narrow at times and really improved once Sane came off. 

Ivory Coast played really well and it was far closer than I thought it would be

Also, the referee was awful
label: analysis

body: zero reason to take a touch there, just shoot the ball, you asked for it, it’s coming to you perfectly, just strike the ball man
label: analysis

body:
Ivory Coast looking really solid tbh, going to be an interesting match up vs either Norway or Senegal, I have them going into RO16.

Just start Undav already. Sane should come off.
label: hot_take

body: Ivory Coast very likely will be haunted by the chances they could not convert in front of the literal goal. They would not lose to Curacao and I doubt Germany would lose to Ecuador so they are very likely the second of the group. They will play runner up of France-Senegal-Norway group while Germany will most likely play a way weaker team. Thats veery unlucky seeding for IC, probably the worst group to pick your opponent from.
label: hot_take

body: As a German, I have to give props to CIV, they looked solid af. Organized and structured on defense, fast and physical on offense. At times it seemed they have 1 man more, always a sign of good coaching & execution. I don’t think Germany played poorly at all and yet a 1-1 would’ve been fair. If they continue like that, I think they can beat Norway/Senegal and go the RO16.
label: analysis

body: as I said, they were great, but lets not forget we had good chances too, and ultimatively we had more xG and that matches my impression from the game aswell.
label: hot_take

body:
No one talks about the real reason Ivory Coast were so good:
  
They have a trainer who understands the German game and countered it perfectly. 
IvC played a strong defense against Germanys strong offense and combined that with strong pressing and fast counters. They didn’t need many people to get chances because Germany placed most of the players high up. And Germany needed many people high up to break through IvC defense. And they stopped the Germans by marking man to man, it shut down a lot of germanies playstile. 

Both teams answered the other tactically and played to win. 

Curasao otoh also played an offensive game but got outclassed by Germany. They could have played defensively and would have condensed a lot less goals but without a chance. Like cap verde, park the bus.
label: analysis

body: Being a German fan, I feel like IVC actually had a few more chances even later they COULD have but did not capitalize on, we did have some luck there. Our defence is still a bit leaky at times. I was also perplexed at the starting 11 choice, when you look at what a physical squad IVC is and happy when the subs finally came in. Much more physical players that the game demanded and we were finally rewarded for it. Nailbiter for sure.
label: analysis

body: It was very good to watch Undav get a chance to kick the ball around a bit before he has to go back to the bench  next game.
label: hot_take

body: If we are back to the old "unconvincing 2nd group stage match" curse from 2006-2014 I'll take it if it also means the success of that era follows!
label: hot_take

body: I believe Kimmich is not convinced of Kimmich as a right back. (Not a dig. I think he’s said he prefers to play centrally)
label: hot_take

body: Germany is gonna go through three teams that can't finish their chances. Good for them to pass, but not necessarily the best preparation for the KOs.
label: hot_take

body: The lack of natural wide players outside of Sane really hurts Germany in games like this but they also lack players who are willing to stretch play with runs in behind.
label: analysis

body: There was a time when I wouldn't have been able to enjoy a win like this, because it would've felt "too undeserved" or whatever. But this game wasn't like that, it was like an open brawl, and I'm really happy that Germany really got in the groove, accepted the physical intensity of the Ivorians and went with it, and got a scrappy win as a reward. Not a technical marvel of a game, but a hard fought win that required resilience and belief.
label: hot_take

body: Ivory Coast were more hungry than Germany I gotta give that too them, especially some of the tackles Ivory initiated were pretty good though some looked like a foul. Germany in the defence was so laid-back, their midfield and forwards did their job of covering up the defence.
label: analysis

body: Their counter-attacks and physical pressing were absolutely no joke. Even though it felt like they kinda got tired in the end. It was a scrappy win for sure, but I'd rather the German team gets tested like this early on in the tournament.
label: hot_take

body: Germany really got lucky here. If they play timid like today they won't go far. They need to be a bit more aggressive and not lose balls this often.
label: hot_take

body: Ivory Coast looked more aggressive and quicker in any 1on1 for most of the time. After the 3 subs for Germany, things turned. Germany got more aggressive while Ivory Coast also looked gassed. They also defended differently compared to their Ecuador game which fucked with Germanys game plan. I would also say that Musiala was the wrong player for this kind of opponent today.
label: analysis

body: "Countless oputiunites" huh... we really didnt watch the same match because their opportunities could easily be counted at two mayyyybe three..
label: hot_take

body: They had a lot of counters especially in the 2nd half. Opportunities (at least for me) isn't always big chances. It's more being able to be threatening in the opposition box. They had great counters, Diallo's technical abilities allowed them to go forward often.
label: analysis

body: This game just exposed the Swedish teams true skill level. In the friendly vs Norway before the WC they were horrible. Lost 3-1, could easily been 7-1.
label: hot_take

body:
Sweden seems kind of naive in the way they play.

Their offensive power looks good on paper, but it's way too easy for halfway decent teams to score against them.
label: hot_take

body: Terrible. Absolute shit. I knew our defense was bad but this is another level. Before match I thought NL would win with 1 or 2 goals but 5-1 is just too bad.
label: hot_take

body: Felt like it was a closer game than the score would imply, although when you think about it, the Netherlands perfectly exploited Sweden’s two low quality defenders (Lagerbielke and Hien). The goals were not ”fluky” at all, and the win with the final score seems quite fair given that Sweden does not have better defenders than that
label: analysis

body: Sweden qualified for this tournament in a typical Scandinavian fashion, lose the qualifiers and then get rewarded with a spot by UEFA
label: hot_take

body:
I really don't understand Potter's tactics in this match, they defended really narrowly and put next to no pressure on the ball, just allowing Holland to cross and come in from the flanks the whole time.  

Then it seems like they switched something around after the first water break, did a lot better than switched back after half time and conceded again. Weird.
label: analysis

body:
This. We played well against Japan. It ended in a draw because of a tactical error by the manager, as well as a superbly motivated Japanese team.

People seem to forget that Japan beat both Germany and Spain in 2022. They’re a highly respectable side.
label: hot_take

body: After the draw with Japan, the Netherlands has made amends today, a result that makes them a serious contender for the cup.
label: hot_take

body: Defense was shit, 3 similar goals in a row and a goalie "error". Sweden would be decent if we just had our defence from olden days.
label: hot_take

body: I can’t lie, I was kind of happy to not face Poland again. Sweden is so bad, it just happened that hey were incredible in their playoff match against you.
label: hot_take

body: Bringing xG in discussion as a Swede is a bit weird, considering you scored 5 with 1.34 against Tunisia. The Dutch had 2.47 against you.
label: hot_take

body: Don't think that's potter just cbs being kinda bad for Sweden and no communication between them. Felt like potter did best he could and it's mostly on back 3 plus goalie this one. We struggled in buildup so Isak whent deeper and suddenly we had a chance
label: analysis

body: Sweden beat both Poland and Ukraine on their way to the World Cup. Also created a shit ton of chances tonight, both teams had abysmal defending but the gk made the biggest difference
label: hot_take

body: I don't see Sweden successfully breaking down their mid-block. All Japan has to do is neutralize your strikers.
label: hot_take

body: It felt like Sweden competed pretty well to me as well, but then I realized that the Netherlands actually defended pretty well imo, most chances Sweden had the Dutch defenders closed the passing lanes and pushed the attackers into positions where they had a low chance to score.
label: analysis

body: The score line doesn’t reflect the game at all. Everything went the Dutch way and we need to learn to not have 40 meters between the back line and the midfield if we are playing a 3-5-2.
label: analysis

body:
We have played a back 3/5 that haven’t been close to feel balanced, either too low or too high and and a general mess. Switched to a back four during the ad break and kept it but sort of fell back into it during pressure. 

Thought it occasionally looked decent with the four which is all you can hope for when doing that kind of adjustment mid-game.
label: analysis

body: Impressed by Brobbey hold up play. Granted its Sweden's defence but did well involving the midfielders and wingers.
label: analysis

body:
Like most European managers, Koeman underestimated the sheer grit of Japan and paid the price. There’s a reason why Japan are always crowd favourites - despite being physically outmatched, they will eat the grass before giving up.

It’s a classic mistake many managers have made. Koeman would never park the bus that early against Spain or France.
label: hot_take

body:
I’m gonna be honest. Initially I expected it to be 5 goals, but after our friendlies and your win against Tunisia I was less sure of it.

After Japan and seeing that Tunisia immediately sacked their manager I was a lot more sure of a win.
label: hot_take

body:
Your problem is going to be getting the ball to your strikers, not the strikers themselves.

Japan is incredibly disciplined and tactically fluid, aimed at neutralizing their opponents danger areas. They'll cut off passing lanes forwards and isolate Isak and Gyokeres.
label: analysis

body: Not a technical player at all, but Dumfries delivers a 100% effort every match for club and country.
label: hot_take

body: It's been looking like this long before this match too. The no pressure on the ball is driving me insane. We can't defend and we make it so much worse by letting world class players play with zero pressure.
label: hot_take

body: You should have seen Sweden before he took over. A Danish bloke had this team completely implode. The players have expressed immense gratitude towards Potter for turning things around, getting them going in the right direction, knowing what to do and say, etc. The fact that we are even here is an insane achievement by Potter in such a short time. Mark my words, barring unlucky injuries, Sweden in Euros 2028 will be dangerous.
label: hot_take

body: if you swap the goalies only the game is probably 3-2 atleast if they learn to defend low cross but yea we didn't but xG and shots says it was alot closer then this. Most of their chances ended in goals
label: analysis

body: Honestly, the USA dragging teams into playing CONCACAF soccer when they have the lead is a pretty damn good idea
label: hot_take

body: I know people are saying it was a more competitive second half - sure, but we were in game management mode after 60 mins and at no point did I see a single shot from the Aussies that looked super dangerous tbh. So all in all I think we won convincingly
label: hot_take

body:
Got to watch the full match after recording it as I couldn't watch it in the afternoon.

It was going to be impossible for the US to match the performance against Paraguay but I don't know how you can come away from today without being impressed. That was a damn solid 1sr half. No Pulisic. McKennie was brilliant. Tillman did one hell of a job creating the free kick with his physicality that led to Freeman's goal. Dest had sequences where he created with some magic.

The 2nd half wasn't anything to write home about. Australia played with good physicality and their best chance was probably the corner where Richards went down. Balogun getting chased down on his chance in the 51st minute or so was probably the best of the bunch for the US. The reality is, you look at other matches where there's an even greater chasm of talent and Brazil "only" beat Haiti, 3-0. 

With Turkey's loss to Paraguay, winning Group D with only two matches played is unbelievably impressive.
label: analysis

body:
Congrats USA! I didnt watch the first game so didnt know what to expect. Your boys are fast and athletic with good positioning.

Australia I love you but midfield needs sorting imo. I feel that no one can control the ball/game.  The panic was definitely felt after the first goal but even then, there is still so much time to level. 

On to Paraguay. Hope Australia wins 💪
label: analysis

body:
Incredibly frustrating game. We were definitely the worse team and deserved to lose but felt like we handicapped ourselves for no reason by not playing any of our biggest threats on the counter. 

We had no way to release pressure in the first half, and when we put on the team that should’ve started, the game was already gone.

Hats off to the US, they played very well, but I can’t help but feel frustrated that we never really got to see our best lineup play at parity.
label: analysis

body: The offside goal? Dude he was clearly onside. And both sides were throwing their bodies around a litttttttttle too much. Both parties got away with fouls early and got called for the same ones late.
label: hot_take

body:
Dest, Tillman, and Robinson were unplayable in the first half, saying both teams had one shot on target is a gross misrepresentation. The own goal doesn't count as a shot on target but it was a high xg chance if balo puts a boot on it. 

You deserved a goal in the second half but the overall result is fair.

I agree that Aus made the USA look average in the second half. Both teams had moments.
label: analysis

body:
Start by saying we (Aus) did not deserve to win.

I will say though, I do not think the US played as well as people are saying. Do keep in mind, both teams went into half time with 1 shot on target each. Though US had a lot of the ball, they really weren't creating much, which was the same when we played Turkiye.

People can over-inflate one team looking better if the other is set-up more defensively like that.

I am baffled that we didn't get a penalty for the handball that occurred when a defender was pushing a jumping Irankunda, but I dont see the accidental trip on Metcalfe as a penalty so not mad at that.

Just overall disappointing first half with a better second half, still not sure why Metcalfe and Irankunda didn't start.
label: analysis

body: It’s weird because anything beyond the R16 seems like it would take a miracle, but anything less would feel like a monumental failure.
label: hot_take

body:
IFAB rules clarifying natural positioning, proximity and ball-to-hand will explain the non-call. Pretty cut and dry when it was waved away INSTANTLY on field and also checked by var. 

Low shots on goal but AUS xG of .44 to U.S.A. 1.3 which shows the USA was more efficient, certainly not dominant by any means even with 70% possession in the first half which is what a lot of people will point to as “dominant”. 

Odd team selection for sure.
label: analysis

body: Thanks, mate.  Your point is an excellent one in that we can’t control the game because of our technical deficiencies, but unfortunately that’s a cultural problem with how we play the game and develop our youth.  We’ll give it a crack though.
label: hot_take

body:
Just way too negative from the start and we were never able to get into the game. If your players can't see a genuine outlet for goal-scoring opportunities, its only a matter of time before you concede.

Hopefully we go through after playing Paraguay and Popovic actually gives his team a chance.
label: hot_take

body:
There is no natural body movement that justifies the arms being outstretched away from the body like that. Pushing an opponent is not a justifiable natural movement.

"By having their arm in such a position, the player takes the risk of their arm being hit by the ball and being penalised".

One can recognise that this was a clear as day handball without needing to believe that the result of the match would have been different if it was given. I can't comprehend the reaching people are doing to justify it. If he jumps for the ball and the arms go out to the side a bit, that's natural. What we saw was unequivocally not a natural position as he did not need to be pushing his opponent to contest that ball.
label: analysis

body:
"touches the ball with their hand/arm when it is in a position that makes their body unnaturally bigger and that position is not the result of their body moving fairly as part of play"

I mean my argument is the way he is pushing the player is not their body moving fairly as part of play, there is a solid amount of outstretch there.
label: hot_take

body:
The US look like the best of the rest (sorta) but miles off the best in the world. They looked pretty bad in the second half and their defense is highly questionable. Very curious what happens when they come up against a team with attacking intent.

I do love that their first thought is to be brave and attack, so many teams in these tournaments just sit back and it's frustrating - and that's exactly what Australia did. They sort of figured it out by the end but it was too late.

I know America is 'murica and people love to meme the whole "wtf is a kilometre" thing, I get it, and have fun. But some takes in here are fucking unbelievable. Give your head a shake. 

Can see them easily getting to the Round of 16 and then being hastily eliminated, but if the draw is kind, R8 is possible. I do think their elimination will be explosive though.
label: hot_take

body: Agreed. Turkey will be an interesting test, but as soon as we come up against a real team (Germany, England, France, etc.) we are in trouble. Of course, weird shit happens all the time..
label: hot_take

body:
>but I dont see the accidental trip on Metcalfe as a penalty so not mad at that.

For me that was clear penalty. 

The Aussie player was in front of the US player, who steps on his achilles

Doesn't really matter if it was accidental, it's still a clear foul in the box

You can compare it with the penalty against Modric where his foul was also accidental
label: analysis

body:
Holy shit, if you think Curacao came here to park the bus you *clearly* haven't watched a minute of their two games so far. God what an ignorant take. They've been attacking every chance they get, and not even through hoofball but with proper high-energy play over the ground.

I get the general sentiment about small-time teams clogging up the roster but this is doing Curacao a great disrespect, they have guts and deserved that point last night. They probably wouldn't have gotten it if Ecuador wasn't so shit at finishing, but these guys played to win. And they could've, because they too missed a few sitters.
label: analysis

body:
Good defence but they are lacking up top, there's a lot of hope for this team in Ecuador because of Caicedo, hincapié and Pacho but there's big gaps. 

I feel like the team has been pretty overrated because of their qualifying performance, and I want Ecuador to do well. The good thing is there's a lot of young talent in Ecuador so the future is bright but this team isn't it.
label: analysis

body: Yea diomande was too much for him. Kimmich is an insane workhorse but he lacks defensive abilities. But the truth is otherwise we would have to bench either Nmecha or Pavlovic + putting someone better on right back which we just don't have.
label: analysis

body:
Adingra should've definitely hit it first time, but his touch truly really bad. He may have gotten a shot off even if he took a touch, but not with the how bad that touch was.

I think because of the angle of the camera, it doesn't capture that Adingra's touch essentially ran back into the German defender.
label: analysis

body:
He is not the profile spain needs against low blocks, spain struggles against low blocks because of their striker profiles 

He had 0 touches against cape verde for first 30 mins didnt he?
label: analysis

body:
Holland scored the same exact goal on Sweden 4 times because Swedish defense was completely shit. 

Iran actually played defensively.

Also those stats are meaningless my dude. Iran would have won this match if their scorer was just a bit more disciplined during their disallowed goal.
label: analysis

body: Good Point, the man marking and the relentless pressing took the wind out of our sails. They got lucky with the ref too. A uefa ref might have called back more of the robust play style. There were no glaring mistakes, but I watched the Japan game later that night and the refereeing difference was pretty obvious. Ivory coast payed the price for the intensity at the end. They were pretty gassed and we had the better subs.
label: analysis

body: The problem is. He did not only park the bus but also took all speed and counterattacking ability out of the attack by subbing if Malen and Summervile for Depay and Koopmeiners. That is stupid regardless of the opponent. It would have been stupid if we played samoa
label: analysis

body: the last coach picked a good route at an unfortunate timing, he saw the writing on the wall, and decided to purge everyone for the young generation, good strategy if you have time to prepare, very bad when you're few months away from the greatest tournament ever! not only he traumatized the kids, hurt the rep of the club, hurt their budget, etc his strategy didn't work, backfired, as a Tunisian, this is heartbreaking!
label: analysis

body: Brazil's last two encounters with the Netherlands ended with Dutch wins. It's amusing to say NL/JPN are unlucky when that's the only really relevant recent h2h. Brazil isn't the Brazil of the '90s/early '00s anymore, and while Morocco is strong, there's still some questions about where they stand exactly at this point. They did struggle quite a bit with Scotland.
label: analysis

body: He decided to only put two players, he should've put at least three, he trusted too much in himself when he's literally a shitty ex-keeper who shouldn't be anywhere near the national team. And on the second goal, you agree with me, I don't care how stupid the defender's pass was, he shouldn't be where he was.
label: analysis

body:
Our midfield has historically been a weakness (we struggle to keep the ball against many teams in Asia that can't even qualify) and to be fair the Turkey result was a big surprise to me. The only thing I can think of was that Turkey were happy to let us try and build from the back after losing possession which hid our deficiencies somewhat, but USA rarely let us keep the ball with their aggressive press.  

There's a reason why we play a low block and go for counters; we can't play through the midfield unless the other team lets us
label: analysis

body:
It just sucks that he's an old man now because he's so calm on the ball and an excellent distributor. Rarely makes mistakes making passes and initiating plays out of the back.

Just wish he had an extra step or two but time is undefeated.
label: analysis
